description: One devoted to learning and study; a person of deep knowledge. 'Ronua' names who studies deeply—mind given to understanding.
sound_symbolism: The word receives with depth. 'Ro' opens with receptive study, knowledge entering. 'Nu' nurtures with accumulated learning, wisdom growing. Then 'a' opens to comprehensive understanding.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo ronua' (the scholar). Deeper than 'ronai' (student)—one who has studied long. Related to 'phewo' (sage) as knowledge to wisdom.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Scholar as seeker—devoted to understanding, not attached to being right.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Scholar as keeper—maintaining and transmitting accumulated knowledge.
